# ecoclaw-mcp

EcoFiClaw core MCP — installed once, owned forever. Gives you 6 tools to manage and improve your AI skills.

## What this is

This MCP server runs locally on your machine and gives Claude access to your installed AI skills. It also lets your skills improve themselves over time based on your feedback — no subscription, no cloud, no ongoing relationship with EcoFiClaw required after install day.

## The 6 tools

### list_skills
Lists all your installed skills. Shows which ones you've customized.

### run_skill
Gets the full prompt for a skill so Claude can use it right now. Always uses your customized version if you have one.

### tune_skill
The powerful one. You say "I want this skill to always use a casual tone" — and the skill's prompt is rewritten to do exactly that, permanently, on your machine. Uses Claude to apply the change intelligently. Original npm version is never touched.

### fork_skill
Creates an independent copy under a new name. Good for specialist variants (e.g. listing-writer-luxury alongside listing-writer).

### contribute
If your improvement would help the community, this submits a GitHub PR to the upstream skill repo. Uses the gh CLI. Completely optional.

### update_skills
Pulls latest npm versions of skills. Skips any skill you've customized — your tuning is always preserved.

## Storage

- `~/.claude/skills/` — npm-installed skills (read-only here)
- `~/.ecoclaw/skills/` — your customizations and forks (writable)
- `~/.ecoclaw/config.json` — your profile and install record
- `~/.ecoclaw/history.jsonl` — log of every tune operation (future rollback)

## Claude Desktop config

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "ecoclaw": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@ecoclaw/mcp"],
      "env": {}
    }
  }
}
```

## Notes for LLMs

- `tune_skill` calls the Anthropic API internally using `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from the environment
- All operations are local — no data leaves the machine except for `contribute` (which submits a GitHub PR) and `tune_skill` (which calls the Anthropic API)
- Skills are plain markdown files (SKILL.md) — human-readable, version-controllable, portable
